_doc: CIM topology C = forward-looking EDGE CIM integrated in a mobile SoC. NO dedicated on-card DRAM: it streams weights from the SoC's SHARED LPDDR5 over the on-chip NoC. The CIM COMPUTE engine (M1, simulator/models/params/m1_cim.json) is topology-agnostic and SHARED with topologies A (Alpha) and B (Card) -- same 800 MHz quad-core AIPU, Card-revalidated. The memory wall is the TARGET LPDDR5 effective BW x a NoC efficiency. It is explicitly NOT the Card's 24.2 GB/s (that is the Card's dedicated on-card LPDDR4x, an older memory + a different topology). UNVALIDATED: no edge silicon exists; noc_efficiency is an ASSUMPTION to calibrate when it does.
honesty: calibrated compute (Alpha 13 pts, Card-revalidated) + ASSUMED edge memory wall (target LPDDR5 sim eff x noc_efficiency; no edge silicon)

notes.memory_wall: eff_BW = mem_lpddr5.eff_BW_GBs (33.3) x noc_efficiency (0.9) = 30.0 GB/s. The 33.3 already includes the LPDDR5 DRAM-controller/system efficiency (0.65 of 51.2 peak); noc_efficiency is the ADDITIONAL on-chip-fabric factor for an integrated CIM sharing LPDDR5. Starts from the memory spec's eff_BW (NOT peak x noc, which would discard it).
notes.not_card_24.2: the Card's 24.2 GB/s is its dedicated on-card LPDDR4x decode wall (topology-specific, older memory); edge uses the SoC's LPDDR5 -> higher BW. Do NOT port 24.2 to edge.
notes.bracket: noc_efficiency in [0.7, 1.0] is a plausible assumption range -> eff_BW in [23.3, 33.3] GB/s; 0.9 (30.0 GB/s) is a mild-NoC-overhead choice.
notes.floor: integrated on-chip NoC -> no per-call PCIe floor (contrast Alpha's measured 911 us).

provenance.noc_efficiency: 0.9 NoC/arbitration overhead for an integrated CIM sharing LPDDR5 [assumption, no edge silicon to calibrate]
provenance.clock_mhz: same 800 MHz AIPU as Alpha/Card [measured, shared compute engine]
